observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant comply with the required periodic reconciliation to sort out the unreconciled differences between their respective records and ascertain the accuracy and existence of the PPE account.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

make a representation to the DA, for the re-funding of unimplemented training and training-related activities, about the YRRP to raise awareness of the program and interest among recipients;

revisit the work plan of the projects and the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement entered into by them in behalf of the LGU and exert effort to complete the implementation of the YRRP in order not to defeat the purpose or objective of the program/project/activities; and

henceforth, require the LGU project in-charge to carefully formulate any project work plan/proposal, specifically on setting an achievable timetable and take into consideration such factors and contingencies that would ensure the timely execution and completion of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to:
 
stop the practice of splitting requisitions to circumvent the requirements of existing laws and regulations and strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR of RA 9184 in all procurements; 

procure office supplies through the Procurement Service of the Department of Budget and Management and secure Procurement Service Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks (CNAS), before procuring from outside suppliers.

submit the lacking documents required in Annex “H”, Appendix A of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001, dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: The same deficiencies were observed on some transactions in the current year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Assessor fast-track the updating of the assessment roll through frequent physical survey. Provided, however, that the necessary technical and manpower support are provided to that Office; and
Municipal Treasurer strategize and strengthen collection drive beyond the required posting and sending out of notices to support and sustain the LGU’s goals and objectives in providing better services and facilities to its constituents
observations.reason: Ongoing update of the assessment roll

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant comply with the required periodic reconciliation to sort out the unreconciled differences between their respective records and ascertain the accuracy and existence of the PPE account; and
Municipal Accountant to fully disclose information in the Notes to Financial Statements regarding the ownership status of the LGU’s landholdings, together with the liens or encumbrances; and
Municipal Assessor proposes to allocate a budget for the preparation of documentary requirements and the undertaking of the necessary courses of action to secure the Land Titles to protect the LGU from possible adverse claims of third parties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Make the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and
Verify the validity of the recorded claims in the prior years of the Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees accounts and undertake the appropriate action as necessary.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer on any pending projects of the LGU funded by the National Government Agencies to ascertain the amount attributable to this dormant account. In case of unavailability of records, we further recommended the filing of a request for the approval of COA to write-off/derecognize from the books the dormant account following the procedures outlined in COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended that the:
LCE direct the HRMO to prepare and submit to the audit team the individual contracts for each JO personnel which contain the specific job description and the specific office to where he/she is assigned and rates daily;
JO personnel are to submit original copies of DTRs and accomplishment reports and original/authenticated copies of logbook/biometric printouts (whichever is applicable) as supporting documents for the payment of their wages and ensure that time entries on DTRs reconcile with the records of attendance as reflected on accomplishment reports.
Personnel-in-charge to review and ensure that the number of days worked indicated in the payroll tallies with the DTR.
LCE/HRMO provide a written justification for the 51 Job Order personnel that were absent during the headcount along with a valid document and identification proving their existence.
We recommended that the LCE ensure that those identified development projects be realized and implemented on time to afford the public of the benefits that can be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Allocate not less than 5% of the total estimated revenue for regular sources for LDRRMF; and
Direct the MDRRMO to judiciously prepare the LDRRMFIP; fully implement all planned programs/projects/activities; and to utilize LDRRMF efficiently to allow the community to realize fully the benefits of the projects/programs in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Budget Officer to strictly monitor the registry on appropriations to avoid excessive expenditures from the established limits in the LDRRMFIP incorporated in the Annual Budget.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Report are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period as required in Paragraph V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, together with the required supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant eliminate the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the preparation of the Consolidated Financial Statements in consonance with accounting rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly ensure that only those allowable charges against the LDRRMF are approved in order to allocate the funds to their intended use and provide the community with the benefits that the fund envisions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider other measures and strategies to complete the project and to strategize the utilization of funds on or before their return to the source agency.  We further recommend the imposition of liquidated damages due to unwarranted delay of the projected implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that future realignments that introduce new PPAs be done by way of an appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget. For existing PPAs, the order for realignment is approved by the proper official who is clothed with authority granted by an ordinance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for each beneficiary barangay and projects to be implemented under the LGSF-SBDP NTF ELCAC to faithfully adhere to the existing rules, regulations, and policies in keeping the records of the Municipality.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Assessor fast-track the updating of the assessment roll through frequent physical survey. Provided, however, that the necessary technical and manpower support are provided to that Office; and
Municipal Treasurer strategize and strengthen collection drive beyond the required posting and sending out of notices to support and sustain the LGU’s goals and objectives in providing better services and facilities to its constituents
observations.reason: Ongoing update of the assessment roll

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant comply with the required periodic reconciliation to sort out the unreconciled differences between their respective records and ascertain the accuracy and existence of the PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant eliminate the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the preparation of the Consolidated Financial Statements in consonance with accounting rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly ensure that only those allowable charges against the LDRRMF are approved in order to allocate the funds to their intended use and provide the community with the benefits that the fund envisions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider other measures and strategies to complete the project and to strategize the utilization of funds on or before their return to the source agency, for other similar projects.  We further recommend the imposition of liquidated damages due to unwarranted delay of the projected implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that future realignments that introduce new PPAs be done by way of an appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget. For existing PPAs, the order for realignment is approved by the proper official who is clothed with authority granted by an ordinance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for each beneficiary barangay and projects to be implemented under the LGSF-SBDP NTF ELCAC to faithfully adhere to the existing rules, regulations, and policies in keeping the records of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the year-end balances of the Cash-in-Bank account and strictly observe the reglementary period of six (6) months for outstanding checks to become stale before they are reverted to Cash-in-Bank account to sustain the settlement of the LGU’s valid obligations

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly set-up the RPT and SET Receivables every beginning of the year and comply with the required preparation of subsidiary ledgers to determine the amounts of deferred income accruing to the LGU and thus achieve reliability of the accounts pertaining to the local taxes
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to achieve reliable and correct balances of the Due to NGAs and Accounts Payable in the financial statements

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide sufficient information in the Notes to give additional value to the financial statements of the LGU for relevant decision-making of its users and other readers
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately update the SCBAA to include the proceeds from borrowings under the Revenue section for a complete and reliable presentation

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly implement the provisions of the Municipal Ordinance regarding payments of the delinquent rentals and surcharges to sustain the local revenue sources beneficial to the LGU

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MGSO on the immediate accomplishment of the required IIRUP and complete the disposal of those unserviceable properties in compliance with the one-time cleansing of the account and obtain higher miscellaneous income from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after its disposal to fairly state the PPE account and afford the LGU of reliable and useful information in decision-making.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects shall be considered in the recapitulation of the Development Projects in the Annual Budget to afford the public with the benefits that can be derived thereat.  Also, enjoin the MPDO and Engineering Unit on the proper coordination during planning period to ascertain that only those viably implementable projects be considered in the list to be funded out from the Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MGSO on the immediate accomplishment of the required IIRUP and complete the disposal of those unserviceable properties in compliance with the one-time cleansing of the account and obtain higher miscellaneous income from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after its disposal to fairly state the PPE account and afford the LGU of reliable and useful information in decision-making.
observations.reason: Unserviceable properties had not yet been disposed and the IIRUP was just submitted recently

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those well-planned and ready-to-procurement-and-implementation-ready projects shall be considered in the recapitulation of the Development Projects in the Annual Budget to afford the public with the benefits that can be derived thereat. Also, enjoin the MPDO and Engineering Unit on the proper coordination during planning period to ascertain that only those viably implementable projects be considered in the list to be funded out from the Development Fund.
observations.reason: Target implementation completion within the first quarter of the ensuing calendar year

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entries to correct the year-end balances of the Cash-in-Bank account and strictly observe the reglementary period of six (6) months for outstanding checks to become stale before they are reverted to Cash-in-Bank account to sustain the settlement of the LGU’s valid obligations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly set-up the RPT and SET Receivables every beginning of the year and comply with the required preparation of subsidiary ledgers to determine the amounts of deferred income accruing to the LGU and thus achieve reliability of the accounts pertaining to the local taxes.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to achieve reliable and correct balances of the Due to NGAs and Accounts Payable in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide sufficient information in the Notes for long-term Loans Payable to give additional value to the financial statements of the LGU for relevant decision-making of its users and other readers.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately update the SCBAA to include the proceeds from borrowings under the Revenue section for a complete and reliable presentation.

1) Municipal Assessor fast-track the updating of the assessment roll through frequent physical survey. Provided, however, that the necessary technical and manpower support are provided to that Office; and
2) Municipal Treasurer strategize and strengthen collection drive beyond the required posting and sending out of notices to support and sustain the LGU’s goals and objectives in providing better services and facilities to its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly implement the provisions of the Municipal Ordinance regarding payments of the delinquent rentals and surcharges to sustain the local revenue sources beneficial to the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately require the Municipal Accountant to communicate and demand from the concerned Barangays the submission of their respective Fund Utilization Report and record liquidation or settlement thereof. Also, ensure that no additional fund transfer be released unless the previous cash advance had been liquidated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Accountant immediately reconcile and make adjusting entries to obtain accurate and fair presentation of the inter-agency reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to consider proper planning and foresight in the procurement of goods to promote economy wherein the most advantageous price for the government is ensured.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe judicious application of the negotiated procurement through emergency purchase modality only on instances or situations where imminent danger to life or property are present to adhere the economic procurement as declared policy of the State.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly ensure that only those allowable charges against the LDRRMF shall be approved so as to direct the funds to its intended use and afford the community the benefits of disaster risk prevention and mitigation, preparedness, rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to include all the crucial information on the Road Networks in the Road Network Ledger Cards and follow the presentation as mandated by Annex D of COA Circular 2015-008 so as to showcase a complete set of financial information for its road networks.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to: a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer and all employees concerned to accelerate the implementation of projects for CY 2020 including all unimplemented prior year projects. b.Convene with the LDC and reevaluate the Municipality’s Priority PPAs and its capacity to implement them with regard to the LGU’s existing manpower, equipment, and finances so as to come-up with a feasible and achievable annual plan.

We recommended management to: a. Enforce the immediate liquidation and refund of these unliquidated cash advances. b.Instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advance to officials and employees with outstanding advances until previous cash advances are fully settled /liquidated.

We recommended management to regularly coordinate with the Local School Board to better formulate the budget and highlight priority to be charged against the SEF. Immediate implementation of the budget during the year is also recommended so as to enable the beneficiaries to enjoy the benefits of the additional educational assistance provided by the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all Project Coordinators involved in the implementation of the project to: 1.Re-evaluate the need to construct / proceed with the unimplemented identified programs and projects stated in the Project Proposal. 2.Fast-track the implementation of the “deemed” beneficial and still feasible projects. 3.Return the whole/part of the amount of the fund to the source agency if the implementation of the project is no longer feasible or necessary.
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a. Require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to prepare the LDRRMFIP
observations.managementAction: b. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF. The LDRRMO shall then submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC to our Office

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to comply strictly with Section 52 of RA No. 10717 or the GAA for Fiscal Year 2016 and that they would not act on additional loan applications unless the P3,500.00 net take home pay is satisfied.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to include all the crucial information on the Road Networks in the Road Network Ledger Cards and follow the presentation as mandated by Annex D of COA Circular 2015-008 so as to showcase a complete set of financial information for its road networks.
observations.reason: To be verified in the 2021 FS

Convene with the LDC and reevaluate the Municipality’s Priority PPAs and its capacity to implement them with regard to the LGU’s existing manpower, equipment, and finances so as to come-up with a feasible and achievable annual plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all Project Coordinators involved in the implementation of the project to:

Re-evaluate the need to construct / proceed with the unimplemented identified programs and projects stated in the Project Proposal.

Fast-track the implementation of the “deemed” beneficial and still feasible projects.

Return the whole / part of the amount of the fund to the source agency if the implementation of the project is no longer feasible or necessary.
observations.reason: Requested to DA for reprogram of the remaining amount

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

Require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to prepare the LDRRMFIP; 
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.  The LDRRMO shall then submit the said report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC to our Office; and
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the separate RAAOs for LDRRMF on or before the 10th day after the end of each month to our Office.
observations.reason: For strict compliance starting 2021

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to comply strictly with Section 52 of RA No. 10717 or the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2016 and that they would not act on additional loan applications unless the P3,500.00 net take home pay is satisfied.
observations.reason: For review in the current year

observations.recommendation: 1.7 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MGSO on the immediate accomplishment of the required IIRUP and complete the disposal of those unserviceable properties in compliance with the one-time cleansing of the account and obtain higher miscellaneous income from the scrap value of those properties. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the unserviceable properties only after its disposal to fairly state the PPE account and afford the LGU of reliable and useful information in decision-making.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive immediately require the Municipal Accountant to communicate and demand from the concerned Barangays the submission of their respective Fund Utilization Report and record liquidation or settlement thereof. Also, ensure that no additional fund transfer be released unless the previous cash advance had been liquidated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile and make adjusting entries to obtain accurate and fair presentation of the inter-agency reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.

4.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to consider proper planning and foresight in the procurement of goods to promote economy wherein the most advantageous price for the government is ensured.

5.10	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe judicious application of the negotiated procurement through emergency purchase modality only on instances or situations where imminent danger to life or property are present to adhere the economic procurement as declared policy of the State.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that only those well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready projects shall be considered in the recapitulation of the Development Projects in the Annual Budget to afford the public with the benefits that can be derived thereat.  Also, enjoin the MPDO and Engineering Unit on the proper coordination during planning period to ascertain that only those viably implementable projects be considered in the list to be funded out from the Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly ensure that only those allowable charges against the LDRRMF shall be approved so as to direct the funds to its intended use and afford the community the benefits of disaster risk prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the GSO to include the medical supplies inventory in the stock cards which he maintains and issue the RIS upon delivery of the same to the RHU for consumption. It is also imperative that the LCE require the GSO to summarize and incorporate the RIS into the RSMI to be submitted to the accountant 5 days after the end of the month to enable the accountant to deduct the issuances in the LGU’s records of existing inventory. 

Direct the GSO, aided by a personnel from the RHU, to conduct a physical count of inventory at year-end and prepare the RPCI, to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor and submit a copy to the Municipal Accountant for her to be able to reconcile the balance per actual count as against LGU records to come up with an accurate measure of inventory at the end of the year.

Instruct the municipal accountant to prepare adjusting entries to record the unrecorded medical supplies and inventories so as to reflect an accurate inventory balance in the FS and henceforth, use the perpetual inventory method of recording all inventory procurements and issuances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries on the unrecorded depreciation expenses on its Road Networks so as to portray an accurate balances of Road Networks in the LGU’s Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all transactions are properly recorded in their appropriate accounts so as to adhere to sound accounting practices and the fair presentation of Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all transactions are properly recorded in their appropriate accounts so as to adhere to sound accounting practices and the fair presentation of Financial Statements

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GSO to hasten the assignment of property numbers and the application of property stickers to the LGU’s PPE so as to ensure that all items of PPE are properly tagged for ease of monitoring.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the LDRRMO/MPDC to use the form as presented in annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the formulation of the LDRRMFIP so as to portray a complete set of data in the monitoring and utilization of current and prior years’ resources which can then be cross referenced with other related documents for future validation.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the form as presented in Annex D of the same circular so as to be thorough in the portrayal of information in the LGU’s Notes to Financial Statements with regards to the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transactions are duly supported with complete supporting documents.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and all employees concerned to use the proper forms in the liquidation of Cash Advances and to present the documents in a systematic and organized manner for purposes of transparency.

We recommended management to follow-up the delivery of the Deeds of Donation or similar documents from their respective donors to facilitate the recording of the unrecorded assets into the LGU’s books. Also, record the corresponding adjustment to the balances of the unrecorded PPE after its recognition.

We recommended management to refrain from spending their budget on road projects constructed on provincial and barangay roads to maximize the utilization of funds for the fulfilment of other priority projects implemented on the municipality’s jurisdiction. Also, the LGU should coordinate with the proper agencies for funds received which intends to implement road projects for road networks other than municipal roads including maintenance and other expenditures after project completion, if any.

Likewise, management should coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and GSO to ensure that there is no double recording of assets from the above-mentioned road network projects.

We recommended management to stop the practice of conducting regular operations under extravagant conditions and be prudent in the disbursement of government funds and avoid granting additional advantages to employees in the performance of regular operations. The Formulation of the APP is a normal function of the office and does not require special conditions for its completion.

We recommended that the LGU abide by the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 requiring the allocation and subsequent spending of no less than 5% of the LGU’s budget to support GAD-related programs and projects to promote gender equality in the workplace and the community.

Furthermore, it is highly recommended that the LGU attribute the cost of gender mainstreaming for regular programs such as procurement of supplies, celebration of foundation day, sports development (male-inclined), and other expenditures which cannot be directly attributed to GAD so that the LGU may have a farer perspective on whether gender equality and responsiveness is being achieved by its current programs and policies by recognizing its equivalent merit using the HGDG tool. Doing so would present more accurately the level of gender responsiveness the municipality is currently at.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the accounts and reports to COA within the period prescribed by existing laws and regulations..

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to revert back the aforementioned balances to Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund and continuously adhere to the Accounting and Reporting Guidelines of LDRRMF set forth by the COA Circular No. 2012-002..

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Mun. Treasurer to take all necessary steps to strengthen the tax collection strategies of the office;

Mun. Assessor to recommend to the sanggunian strategies relating to tax ordinances and revenue measures for the collection of RPT; and,

Sangguniang Bayan of the municipality to support the Mun. Assessor and the Mun. Treasurer in strengthening the LGU’s collection strategies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct officials concerned to strictly enforce the provisions as mandated by Ordinance No 06, s. of 2000 pursuant to Republic Act No 9003, s. of 2000 and other relevant Implementing Rules and Regulations.

It is also recommended that the LGU be innovative and decisive in the imposition of collections and other forms of revenue which may be able to supplement the LGU’s financial needs so as not to end up utilizing funds intended for other legal purposes.

We recommended that the LGU abide by the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 requiring the allocation and subsequent spending of no less than 5% of the LGU’s budget to support GAD-related programs and projects to promote gender equality in the workplace and the community.

Furthermore, it is highly recommended that the LGU attribute the cost of gender mainstreaming for regular programs such as procurement of supplies, celebration of foundation day, sports development (male-inclined), and other expenditures which cannot be directly attributed to GAD so that the LGU may have a farer perspective on whether gender equality and responsiveness is being achieved by its current programs and policies by recognizing its equivalent merit using the HGDG tool. Doing so would present more accurately the level of gender responsiveness the municipality is currently at.

We recommended management to regularly coordinate with the Local School Board to better formulate the budget and highlight expenditures warranting due priority to be charged against the SEF. Immediate implementation of the budget during the year is also recommended so as to enable the beneficiaries to enjoy the benefits of the additional educational assistance provided by the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: The LGU’s main concern for 2020 was the implementation of PPAs for COVID response, thus, the PPAs for SEF was delayed.

observations.recommendation: Implement the Development Projects enumerated in the 20% component of the Annual Investment Plan effectively and efficiently to deliver desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the public or community.
observations.reason: Prohibitions and observance of health protocols due to COVID19

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct officials concerned to strictly enforce the provisions as mandated by Ordinance No 06, s. of 2000 pursuant to Republic Act No 9003, s. of 2000 and other relevant Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommend management to comply strictly with Section 52 of RA No. 10717 or the General Appropriations Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2016 and that they would not act on additional loan applications unless the P3,500.00 net take home pay is satisfied.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officials strictly enforce the provisions mandated by Ordinance No. 06, s. of 2000 pursuant to Republic Act No. 9003 and other relevant Implementing Rules and Regulations. It is also recommended that the LGU be innovative and decisive in the imposition of collections and other forms of revenue which may be able to supplement the LGU’s financial needs so as not to end up utilizing funds intended for other legal purposes.
observations.reason: No collections imposed

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to follow-up the delivery of the Deeds of
Donation or similar documents from their respective donors to facilitate the
recording of the unrecorded assets into the LGU’s books. Also, record the
corresponding adjustment to the balances of the unrecorded PPE after its
recognition.

We recommended management to refrain from spending their budget on
road projects constructed on provincial and barangay roads to maximize the
utilization of funds for the fulfilment of other priority projects implemented on
the municipality’s jurisdiction. Also, the LGU should coordinate with the
proper agencies for funds received which intends to implement road projects
for road networks other than municipal roads including maintenance and other
expenditures after project completion, if any.

Likewise, management should coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and
GSO to ensure that there is no double recording of assets from the above-
mentioned road network projects.

We recommended management to:

a. Enforce the immediate liquidation and refund of these unliquidated
cash advances.

b. Instruct the Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash
advance to officials and employees with outstanding advances until
previous cash advances are fully settled /liquidated.

We recommended management to stop the practice of conducting regular
operations under extravagant conditions and be prudent in the disbursement
of government funds and avoid granting additional advantages to employees in
the performance of regular operations. The Formulation of the APP is a
normal function of the office and does not require special conditions for its
completion.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the accounts and reports to COA within the period prescribed by existing laws and regulations

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389, dated Nov. 3, 1992, maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of public funds. The perforation or stamping of paid vouchers including their supporting documents is an internal control procedure on the prevention of presenting the same documents in the future for claims of the same nature.
We recommend that the BAC let the agency resort to an alternative method of procurement, and endorse by adopting RULE XVI, Section 48 of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We Recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: a. Require the LDRRMC to prepare the monthly reports of the LDRRMFIP; b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly report on sources and utilization of the DRRMF. The LDRRMO shall then submit the said report before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LCD to our office; and c. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the separate RAAOs for the LDRRMF on or before the end of each month to our office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to revert back the aforementioned balances to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund and continuously adhere to the accounting and Reporting Guidelines set forth by COA Circular No 2012-02.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the LSB to expedite the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects, activities in the SEF budget pursuant to Sections 99(a), 100(c) and 272 of RA 7160 and adhere to the guidelines in Planning and Budgeting for the SEF pursuant to Section 5.5 of DepEd, DBM and DILG JMC No. 1, s. 2017; and b. Cause close coordination among those officials/employees involved in planning, execution, and monitoring to maximize utilization and speed up its implementation per Annual SEF Budget to ensure that intended beneficiaries could enjoy optimum benefits at earliest possible time.
observations.managementAction: Mayor directed the LSB to expedite the implementation of the prioritized SEF Budget.
observations.reason: Implementation of projects still delayed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. Direct the Head of implementing offices to expedite the implementation of the aforementioned projects in accordance with its corresponding MOA; b. Return any unutilized funds to source agencies as specified in the MOA.
observations.reason: Numerous projects from NGAs are still unimplemented to-date / No unutilized funds under the fund transfers were returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the: Mun. Treasurer to take all necessary steps to strengthen the tax collection strategies of the office; Mun. Assessor to recommend to the sanggunian strategies relating to tax ordinances and revenue measures for the collection of RPT; and, Sangguniang Bayan of the municipality to support the Mun. Assessor and the Mun. Treasurer in strengthening the LGU’s collection strategies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer issued demand letters to delinquent tax payers.
observations.reason: Tax collections still not maximized / lacking

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provisions provided by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and other relevant Implementing Rules and Regulations in the preparation of the Annual Budget and the implementation of the AIP. Refrain from charging personnel benefits, operating expenses, and administrative costs into the 20% Development Fund so as to ensure the funds are optimally utilized solely for the development of the municipality.

We recommend that management comply strictly with Section 52 of RA No. 10717 or the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2016 and that they would not act on additional loan applications unless the P3,500.00 net take home pay is satisfied.
observations.reason: Unexpired loan periods of the borrowers affected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management expedite the Implementing process/procurement to effectively deliver the development projects embodied in the 20% Annual Investment Plan to avoid depriving the community of the desirable socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.
observations.reason: Due to delayed bidding process, procurement component of the process and failure in implementation of previous backlogs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
require the disbursing officer to maintain a Cashbook for all the cash advances
he received in his capacity as a Disbursing Officers.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned employee to
liquidate immediately his outstanding cash advances after the purpose for which it was
given has been served.

We recommend that the Muncipal Mayor direct the:
a. Mun. Treasurer to take all necessary steps to strengthen the tax collection
strategies of the office;
b. Mun. Assessor to recommend to the sanggunian strategies relating to tax
ordinances and revenue measures for the collection of RPT; and,
c. Sangguniang Bayan of the municipality to support the Mun. Assessor and the
Mun. Treasurer in strengthening the LGU’s collection strategies.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer
to refrain from charging BAC and TWG Honoraria other than those from the collections
of funds related to the said purpose. Adhere strictly to the provisions of the Department
of Budget and Management (DBM) Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit the lacking documents and comply with the requirements of (1) IRR of RA 9184,
(2) COA Circular No. 92-389 and (3) PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer
and Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provisions as provided by DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and other relevant Implementing Rules and
Regulations in the preparation of the Annual Budget and the implementation of the AIP.
Refrain from charging personnel benefits, operating expenses, and
administrative costs into the 20% Development Fund so as to ensure the funds are
optimally utilized solely for the development of the municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct officials concerned to strictly
enforce the provisions as mandated by Ordinance No 06, s. of 2000 pursuant to Republic
Act No 9003, s. of 2000 and other relevant Implementing Rules and Regulations.
It is also recommended that the LGU be innovative and decisive in the imposition
of collections and other forms of revenue which may be able to supplement the LGU’s
financial needs so as not to end up utilizing funds intended for other legal purposes.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer and Local Project Officer to submit justifications
on the delayed implementation of the project;
b. Direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor closely all projects of the LGU and in
case of delay in implementation by any contractor, prepare a catch up plan to put
the project back in schedule;
c. Execute all administrative, legal remedies and actions against a defaulting
contractor.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct personnel concerned to
regularly coordinate with the Local School Board to better formulate the budget and
highlight expenditures warranting due priority to be charged against the SEF to
optimize the utilization of the fund. Stop charging eligible school expenditures to the
20% development fund or to any other appropriations but only to SEF.

Full utilization of the SEF is recommended for expenditures relating to items
mentioned under Section 272 of RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No.1 s, 2017
instead of charging the same into the 20% Development Fund or any other fund
intended for other legal purposes.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
appropriate 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for programs, projects and
activities of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC).
We recommend that Management refrain from charging job order wages out of the 20% Development Fund. Adhere strictly to the provisions of the DILG and DBM Joint memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommend that:
 The Municipal Accountant make appropriate journal entries to transfer balances of current and previous years of LDRRMF from the General Fund to the special trust fund
 The Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO makes proper reporting of the funds; and,
 Management observe strict maintenance of the DRRM Funds and proper charging from it.

We recommend management to comply strictly with Section 52 of RA No. 10717 or the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2016 and that they would not act on additional loan applications unless the P3,500.00 net take home pay is satisfied.

We recommend that:
1.Serve demand letters to those officials and employees, elective and appointive, requiring them to settle or liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances otherwise, suspend their salaries;
2.Stop the practice of granting cash advances for travels of officials and employees with outstanding and unliquidated cash advances; and
3.Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger cards for each official/employee who are granted cash advances for monitoring purposes.
Stop the practice of charging the loan amortization and other expense items not related to development projects from the 20% Economic Development Fund.

Immediately submit to the Audit Team the valid official receipts/sales invoice as legal evidence of the transactions. Henceforth, Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 51-2013 dated August 13, 2013 should be strictly followed in all future purchases.

Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous recording of the specific transactions as aforementioned and, considering that the deficiency relates only to errors in recording and there being no incipient pecuniary loss, the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance may not be necessary.
observations.reason: Considerin g that the deficiency relates only to errors in recording and there being no incipient pecuniary loss, the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance may not be necessary.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to pay only those claims supported with complete and appropriate documents as provided under Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree 1445. Require the concerned claimants to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents as required under Section COA Circular 2012-001. The issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance may be consequently done by the Team in the event that the required documents, as herein enumerated, are not satisfactorily submitted by management within a reasonable time.
observations.reason: The issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance may be consequently done by the Team in the event that the required documents, as herein enumerated, are not satisfactorily submitted by management within a reasonable time.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of charging the loan amortization for the LGU’s service vehicle and other expense items that are not related to development projects from the 20% Economic Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Loan amortization for 2016 has already been exhausted early that year. This recommendation will be implemented on 2017.
observations.reason: The Capital Outlay Budget for 2016 has already been exhausted early that year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the LCE direct the Municipal
Accountant to comply strictly with Section 52 of RA No. 10717 or the
General Appropriations Act (GAA) for Fiscal Year 2016 and that they would not act
on additional loan applications unless the P3,500.00 net take home pay is
satisfied.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to refrain from charging job order wages out of the 20%
Development Fund. Adhere strictly to the provisions of the DILG and DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommend that:
 The Municipal Accountant make appropriate journal entries to
transfer unexpended balances of current and previous years
LDRRMF from the General Fund to the special trust fund
account, "Trust Liability-DRRM";
 The Municipal Accountant and MDRRMO makes proper
reporting of the funds; and,
 Management observe strict maintenance of the DRRM Funds and
proper charging from it.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to implement the audit recommendation and prepare adjusting entries to: a) Record the appropriate adjusting entries of the loans granted by the LBP, Borongan Branch in 2007, 2008 and 2011, to record the outstanding balance of the Loan granted by the LBP, Borongan Branch in 2007, 2008 and 2011; b) Record the Infrastructures/Projects financed by the Loans and the related depreciation; c) Correct the overstated balance of the Financial Expenses; d) Correct the understated balance of the Government Equity; e) ; f) Further, submit explanation/justification for the non-compliance of audit recommendation for almost 3 years.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant already draw reconcile records reflecting the outstanding balance of the Loan and related depreciation. Record the outstanding balance of the Loan Infrastructures/Projects financed by the Loans and related depreciation.

observations.recommendation: Require the Local Chief Executive to: (a) require the Municipal General Services Officer to submit the Physical Inventory Report; (b) maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant.
observations.managementAction: GSO already submitted an updates Physical Inventory Report.

observations.recommendation: should: a) Serve demand letters to those officials and employees, requiring them to settle or liquidate the outstanding cash advances immediately otherwise, suspend their salaries until full liquidation has been made; b) Stop the practice of granting cash advances for travels of officials and employees with outstanding and unliquidated cash advances; and c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain individual subsidiary ledger cards for each official/employee who are granted cash advances for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: Mgmt. served demand letters to those officials and employees, requiring them to settle or liquidate the outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: There were still two (2) Cash Advances that were not liquidated because the employee was already connected with the agency.

observations.recommendation: that the Mayor submit written justifications with supporting documents why the service vehicle was procured through a loan agreement; submit to the Audit Team a copy of the Approval of the DILG Secretary for the purchase of the service vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor submit written justifications with supporting documents and copy of the Approval of the DILG Secretary.

observations.recommendation: Stop the additional procurement of unnecessary expenditures relating thereto, which could be fully utilized on projects beneficial to the public as a whole.
observations.managementAction: Procure items which are available within the locality to avoid additional unnecessary expenditures relating thereto.

observations.recommendation: Implement the Projects enumerated in the 20% component of the Annual Investment Plan effectively and efficiently to deliver desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the benefit of the public or community.
observations.managementAction: Implemented focused on its Solid Waste Management effectively and efficiently to accomplish major component of the 20% development component of the Annual Investment Plan. Seven more projects were not completed due to time constraint. Such will be implemented on 2017.
observations.reason: There were still seven more projects which were not completed due to time constraint; will be implemented on 2017.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain properly the related subsidiary ledgers and prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliations for all bank accounts pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. 1445 and Sec. 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011, and submit them to the Audit Team within the reglementary period for verification purposes.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Municipal Accountant to maintain properly related subsidiary ledgers and prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliations for all bank accounts and submit to the Audit Team within the reglementary period for verification purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the: (a) Municipal General Services Officer to submit the report of the physical inventory report of the PPE every year as mandated by law, and to maintain and update the property cards and reconcile the records with the Accountant; (b) Management should set up depreciation of all depreciable properties of the agency; and (c) Inventory Team to conduct a complete count of all properties of the Province including those of the devolved agencies in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Management implemented the Physical Inventory report and submitted the report.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive submit immediately all the stated required documents to the Chairman of the Commission on Audit in a sealed envelope for auditing purposes, copy furnished the Audit Team. Henceforth, strictly follow/comply the provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-003.
observations.managementAction: AAR, that the Local Chief Executive submit immediately all stated required documents to the Chairman of the Commission on Audit in a sealed envelope for auditing purposes, copy furnished the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documents immediately to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Henceforth, the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014, be strictly followed/comply. Likewise, refrain from processing claims which are not supported with complete documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit pertaining to the lacking documents to the Audit Team for audit. Henceforth, management should see to it that the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445, relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014, be strictly followed.
observations.reason: Management already submitted the required documents pertaining to the disbursements totalling P652,728.91.

observations.recommendation: Immediately refund excess claims for travels and submit to the Audit Team copies of the Official Receipt issued by the Municipal Treasurer as evidence of payment and the corresponding deposit slips.
observations.managementAction: AAR refund excess claims for travels and submit copies of the Official Receipt issued by the Mun. Treasurer as evidence of payment and the corresponding deposit slips.
observations.reason: The Official Receipt issued by the Mun. Treasurer as evidence of payment and the corresponding deposit slips were provided. Henceforth, the provisions of EO 298 for payments of travels should be strictly followed.

observations.recommendation: that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all the subject financial reports with the corresponding supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification purposes. Henceforth, management should ensure that provisions of Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of RA 7160 are strictly adhered to through regular and close supervision of all concerned personnel/officers.
observations.managementAction: AAR that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the subject financial reports with the corresponding supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification purposes.

observations.recommendation: Immediately submit to the Audit Team valid official Sales Invoices/official receipts/sales invoice as legal evidence of the transactions. Henceforth, Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 51-2013 should be strictly followed in all future purchases.
observations.managementAction: AAR, submit to the Audit Team the required valid official Sales Invoices/sales receipt as evidence of the transactions.
